boundaryRules.name: no-circular
boundaryRules.comment: No runtime circular dependencies — they defeat the layered design. Type-only cycles are allowed: TypeScript erases them (e.g. the Pattern <-> KeyboardIR type cross-reference in contracts).

boundaryRules.name: lint-not-to-engine
boundaryRules.comment: Layer C (@keymanapp/keyboard-lint) must not import the engine (CLAUDE.md / spec §10). Lint is a standalone hygiene layer.

boundaryRules.name: engine-not-to-studio
boundaryRules.comment: Engine must not import the studio SPA — the engine is upstream of the UI (spec §12/§13 team boundaries). Dependencies flow studio -> engine.

boundaryRules.name: contracts-is-the-dependency-root
boundaryRules.comment: contracts is the dependency root — everything builds to it, so it must not import any other workspace package (CLAUDE.md).

boundaryRules.name: ui-is-a-leaf
boundaryRules.comment: studio ui/ primitives are a dependency leaf: no imports from survey/, steps/, or stores/ (feature 011).

boundaryRules.name: editors-no-dashboard
boundaryRules.comment: editors/ may import stores/ and lib/ (galleries bind workingCopyStore, irToCarveNodes, buildTouchLayoutJson — FR-007 / P4a boundaries.contract.md). Forbidden: editors/ -> dashboard/ (editors are steps, not orchestrators).

boundaryRules.name: steps-layer
boundaryRules.comment: steps/ orchestrates editor steps and question modules. It may depend on survey/ (registry), editors/, contracts, and ui/. Forbidden: steps/ -> dashboard/, stores/, lib/, components/ (steps is a descriptor layer, not a UI consumer — P4a boundaries.contract.md).

boundaryRules.name: dashboard-layer
boundaryRules.comment: dashboard/ reads the step manifest and survey/IR types. It may depend on steps/, contracts, and ui/. Forbidden: dashboard/ -> editors/ or stores/ directly (dashboard orchestrates via steps, not by touching editor internals — P4a boundaries.contract.md).

boundaryRules.name: question-modules-no-bypass-mutate-seam
boundaryRules.comment: survey/questions/ modules must be PURE descriptors: they declare inputs/writes and a pure mutate(value, ctx) that returns a Partial<KeyboardIR> patch (spec-014 mutate-seam, FR-002/-005). They MUST NOT write the working copy directly — no imports of stores/, editors/, or lib/. The single executed IR write path is mutate() applied by the reducer (steps/reducer.ts -> steps/mutateApply.ts). Importing the store or an editor from a question module would re-open the answer-store-vs-IR state fork P5 closes (SC-001).

boundaryRules.name: no-deps-on-studio-poc
boundaryRules.comment: studio-poc is a throwaway prototype — do not build on it (CLAUDE.md). Nothing outside studio-poc may import it.
